title: Exploring cell-cell interaction with blisa
text: blisa identifies spatially enriched ligand-receptor (LR) interactions spatial transcriptomics data using bivariate Local Moran’s (LISA) statistics. core idea bin cells hexagonal grid, compute spatial co-enrichment every ligand-receptor pair across bins, flag “High-High” hotspot bins partners spatially co-expressed beyond chance. vignette walks typical workflow Xenium breast cancer dataset.

title: Exploring cell-cell interaction with blisa
code: library(blisa) library(SpatialExperiment)

title: Exploring cell-cell interaction with blisa
text: example dataset small SpatialExperiment object (one Xenium breast cancer section) hosted GitHub Release asset. Download cache locally:
code: data_url   <- "https://github.com/ChenLaboratory/example_data/releases/download/v1.0.0/spe_xenium_bc_s1rep1.rds" cache_dir  <- tools::R_user_dir("blisa", "cache") data_file  <- file.path(cache_dir, "spe_xenium_bc_s1rep1.rds")  if (!file.exists(data_file)) {   dir.create(cache_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)   download.file(data_url, data_file, mode = "wb") }  spe <- readRDS(data_file) spe #> class: SpatialExperiment  #> dim: 313 163797  #> metadata(0): #> assays(1): counts #> rownames(313): ABCC11 ACTA2 ... ZEB2 ZNF562 #> rowData names(3): ID Symbol Type #> colnames(163797): cell_1 cell_2 ... cell_167779 cell_167780 #> colData names(12): cell_id transcript_counts ... gene_counts cell_type #> reducedDimNames(0): #> mainExpName: NULL #> altExpNames(0): #> spatialCoords names(2) : x_centroid y_centroid #> imgData names(1): sample_id

title: Exploring cell-cell interaction with blisa
text: hexBinCells() aggregates cells hexagonal bins. group argument supplied (, cell type), also returns per-cell-type bin matrices counts_by_group, needed downstream CCI scoring spatial visualisation.
code: coords <- as.data.frame(SpatialExperiment::spatialCoords(spe)) counts <- SummarizedExperiment::assay(spe, "counts")  binned <- hexBinCells(   coords_df     = coords,   counts_matrix = counts,   bin_size      = 50,   group         = spe$cell_type )  # Components: #   binned$counts_matrix   - gene x bin sparse matrix (all cells) #   binned$bins            - sf polygons (with n_cells column) #   binned$counts_by_group - named list of gene x bin matrices, one per cell type

title: Exploring cell-cell interaction with blisa
text: blisa() everything one call: spatial weights, LR pair filtering CellChatDB, bivariate Moran’s per LR pair, hotspot identification, (counts_by_group supplied) cell-cell interaction scoring. result blisa object four slots: LR_results — one row per LR pair, sorted number hotspot bins bins — hexagonal grid sf object spatial_weights — queen distance-decay weights computeSpatialWeights() CCI_scores — wide data frame sender-receiver interaction scores per LR pair
code: res <- blisa(   binned$counts_matrix,   bins            = binned$bins,   n_cells_col     = "n_cells",   counts_by_group = binned$counts_by_group ) #> Downloading CellChatDB.human from GitHub (once per session)... #> Testing 12 LR pairs... #>   |========================================| 100%  res #> A blisa object #>  LR pairs tested  : 12  #>  Significant pairs: 12  #>  Bins             : 17215  #>  CCI computed     : TRUE

what: Rank LR Pairs by Hotspot Count
title: Exploring cell-cell interaction with blisa
code: plotLRrank(res, top = 30)

title: Exploring cell-cell interaction with blisa
text: chosen LR pair (, top-ranked pair default), shows bins significant hotspots, coloured p-value LISA score.
code: plotHotspots(res, index = 1) # Or by gene names: # plotHotspots(res, ligand = "CXCL12", receptor = "CXCR4")

title: Exploring cell-cell interaction with blisa
text: Rows sender→receiver cell-type pairs; columns LR pairs.  Filter specific senders receivers:
code: plotCCI(res, top_lr = 20, top_pairs = 30) plotCCI(res,         sender = c("CD4+_T_Cells", "CD8+_T_Cells", "B_Cells",                    "Macrophages", "DCs"),         receiver = c("Invasive_Tumor", "DCIS", "Myoepi") )

title: Exploring cell-cell interaction with blisa
code: plotCCILR(res, ligand = "CXCL12", receptor = "CXCR4")

title: Exploring cell-cell interaction with blisa
text: Sender × receiver heatmap scores summed (user-supplied function) across LR pairs:
code: plotCCIsummary(res) # With a different aggregation: # plotCCIsummary(res, agg_fun = mean)

what: Spatial Map of Dominant Cell-Type Pairs
title: Exploring cell-cell interaction with blisa
text: hotspot bin chosen LR pair, identifies dominant sender→receiver cell-type combination based ligand expression neighbourhood receptor expression inside bin.
code: plotCCIspatial(   res,   counts_by_group = binned$counts_by_group,   index = 1 )

what: Infer Cell-Cell Communication from Spatial Transcriptomics
title: Infer Cell-Cell Communication from Spatial Transcriptomics
text: blisa implements functionalities study cell-cell communication spatial transcriptomics experiments using bivariate local indicator spatial autocorrelation. Install development version GitHub
code: library(devtools) devtools::install_github("ChenLaboratory/blisa")

what: blisa: Bivariate Local Indicator of Spatial Association for spatial transcriptomics — blisa-package
title: blisa: Bivariate Local Indicator of Spatial Association for spatial transcriptomics — blisa-package
text: Identifies cell-cell communication hotspots spatial transcriptomics data using bivariate Local Moran's statistics hexagonally binned cells. Provides functions spatial weighting, ligand-receptor pair filtering, hotspot detection, visualisation sender-receiver cell-type interactions.

title: blisa: Bivariate Local Indicator of Spatial Association for spatial transcriptomics — blisa-package
text: Maintainer: Yunshun Chen yuchen@wehi.edu.au Authors: Lei Qin qin.l@wehi.edu.au Lizhong Chen

what: Run BLISA spatial cell-cell communication analysis — blisa
title: Run BLISA spatial cell-cell communication analysis — blisa
text: Generic function running BLISA (Bivariate Local Indicator Spatial Association). Dispatches class x: blisa.default accepts pre-binned gene--bin count matrix matching bins polygon object. blisa.SpatialExperiment accepts cell-level SpatialExperiment object bins cells hexagonal tiles internally via hexBinCells running analysis.

title: Run BLISA spatial cell-cell communication analysis — blisa
code: blisa(x, ...)  # Default S3 method blisa(   x,   bins,   LR_df = NULL,   bin_size = 50,   dmax = 250,   nsim = 999,   p_cutoff = 0.05,   min_ligand = 10,   min_receptor = 10,   min_cells = 1,   n_cells_col = NA,   annotation_col = "annotation",   default_mode = "diffuse",   diffuse_category = c("Secreted Signaling", "Non-protein Signaling"),   species = c("human", "mouse"),   genes = NULL,   counts_by_group = NULL,   fast = TRUE,   cpu_threads = 4L,   verbose = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'SpatialExperiment' blisa(   x,   bin_size = 50,   LR_df = NULL,   group = "cell_type",   genes = NULL,   min_cells = 1,   min_total_counts = 10,   verbose = FALSE,   ... )

title: Run BLISA spatial cell-cell communication analysis — blisa
text: x gene--bin count matrix (blisa.default) cell-level SpatialExperiment object (blisa.SpatialExperiment). ... Additional arguments passed relevant method. bins sf object bin polygons. Row order must match column order x. LR_df Data frame ligand-receptor pairs columns ligand.symbol receptor.symbol. NULL, CellChatDB chosen species downloaded automatically. bin_size Numeric. Width hexagonal bin coordinate units (e.g. microns). Passed hexBinCells computeSpatialWeights. Default 50. dmax Numeric. Maximum distance diffuse-mode neighbours. Default 250. nsim Integer. Number permutations Moran's significance. Default 999. p_cutoff Numeric. P-value threshold High-High hotspots. Default 0.05. min_ligand Numeric. Minimum ligand count threshold. Default 10. min_receptor Numeric. Minimum receptor count threshold. Default 10. min_cells Integer. Bins fewer cells dropped binning hexBinCells. Default 1. n_cells_col Character NA. Column bins holding per-bin cell counts used min_cells filtering. Set NA skip (default). annotation_col Character. Column LR_df specifying interaction category used communication-mode assignment. Default "annotation". default_mode Character. CCC mode assigned LR pairs whose annotation match diffuse_category. Default "diffuse". diffuse_category Character vector annotation categories treated diffuse signalling. species Character. CellChatDB download LR_df = NULL. One "human" (default) "mouse". genes Character vector gene names consider matching ligand-receptor pairs. Defaults rownames(x) (genes SpatialExperiment object). counts_by_group Named list gene--bin count matrices, one per group level (e.g. cell type), returned hexBinCells group supplied. provided, runCCI called automatically BLISA loop output included result CCI_scores. Default NULL. fast Logical. TRUE (default), uses fastLISA::local_moran_bv (fast C/OpenMP backend) bivariate local Moran's computation. FALSE, uses original spdep::localmoran_bv + spdep::hotspot pipeline. cpu_threads Integer. Number OpenMP threads used fastLISA::local_moran_bv. used fast = TRUE. Ignored platforms without OpenMP. Default 4L. verbose Logical. TRUE, print progress messages. Default FALSE (silent). group Character. Column name colData(x) use grouping variable (e.g. cell type) per-group bin aggregation downstream CCI analysis via runCCI. column found colData(x), message issued CCI skipped. Default "cell_type". min_total_counts Numeric. Bins whose total counts (summed genes) fall threshold dropped binning hexBinCells. Set 0 disable. Default 10.

title: Run BLISA spatial cell-cell communication analysis — blisa
text: list; see individual method documentation details. object class blisa four components: LR_results Data frame BLISA results LR pair, including ccc_mode, sig_numbers, sig_index, sig_pval, all_pval, all_lisa, all_quadrant, original columns LR_df. all_quadrant character vector hotspot quadrant labels ("High-High", "Low-Low", etc.) every bin; non-tested bins NA. bins Bin-level sf object hexagonal polygons. spatial_weights Spatial weights list computeSpatialWeights. CCI_scores NULL unless counts_by_group supplied, case wide data frame interaction scores runCCI: rows "Sender->Receiver" group pairs, columns LR pairs.

title: Run BLISA spatial cell-cell communication analysis — blisa
text: blisa(default): Method gene--bin count matrix. blisa(SpatialExperiment): Method cell-level SpatialExperiment object. Bins cells hexagonal tiles via hexBinCells delegates blisa.default.

title: Run BLISA spatial cell-cell communication analysis — blisa
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Download and cache the example Xenium breast cancer dataset (~21 MB) data_url  <- paste0(   "https://github.com/ChenLaboratory/example_data/releases/",   "download/v1.0.0/spe_xenium_bc_s1rep1.rds" ) cache_dir <- tools::R_user_dir("blisa", "cache") data_file <- file.path(cache_dir, "spe_xenium_bc_s1rep1.rds") if (!file.exists(data_file)) {   dir.create(cache_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)   download.file(data_url, data_file, mode = "wb") } spe <- readRDS(data_file)  # blisa.SpatialExperiment method: bins cells, runs LISA, scores CCI result <- blisa(spe, bin_size = 50, group = "cell_type") result } # }

what: Compute Spatial Weights for BLISA — computeSpatialWeights
title: Compute Spatial Weights for BLISA — computeSpatialWeights
text: Builds queen (nearby) distance-decay (diffuse) spatial weight matrices bin-level sf object, excluding isolated bins optionally excluding low-cell bins. second-pass isolation check removes bins become isolated initial subset.

title: Compute Spatial Weights for BLISA — computeSpatialWeights
code: computeSpatialWeights(   bins,   bin_size = 50,   dmax = 250,   min_cells = 1,   n_cells_col = NA )

title: Compute Spatial Weights for BLISA — computeSpatialWeights
text: bins sf object spatial bins. bin_size Numeric. Bin spacing used define queen adjacency (1.2 * bin_size radius). dmax Numeric. Maximum distance diffuse-mode neighbours. min_cells Integer. Minimum cell count bin included. Ignored n_cells_col = NA. n_cells_col Character NA. Column name bins holding per-bin cell counts. Set NA skip cell-count filtering (default).

title: Compute Spatial Weights for BLISA — computeSpatialWeights
text: list : queen_wt Spatial weights list nearby (queen) mode. dist_wt Spatial weights list diffuse (distance-decay) mode. keep_idx_queen Integer indices bins used queen-mode Moran. keep_idx_dist Integer indices bins used diffuse-mode Moran. isolate_idx_queen Integer indices original queen-mode isolates. isolate_idx_dist Integer indices original diffuse-mode isolates. low_cell_idx Integer indices bins excluded low cell counts. queen_nb_full Full (unsubset) neighbour list nearby mode, indexed bins. dist_nb_full Full (unsubset) neighbour list diffuse mode, indexed bins.

title: Compute Spatial Weights for BLISA — computeSpatialWeights
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(42) pts  <- sf::st_as_sf(   data.frame(x = runif(300, 0, 1000), y = runif(300, 0, 1000)),   coords = c("x", "y"), crs = NA ) bins <- sf::st_sf(   geometry = sf::st_make_grid(pts, cellsize = 100,                               what = "polygons", square = FALSE) ) sw <- computeSpatialWeights(bins, bin_size = 100, dmax = 300) names(sw) } # }

what: Filter ligand-receptor pairs by expression threshold — filterLRpairs
title: Filter ligand-receptor pairs by expression threshold — filterLRpairs
text: Retains LR pairs least one bin/spot counts min_ligand every ligand subunit min_receptor every receptor subunit.

title: Filter ligand-receptor pairs by expression threshold — filterLRpairs
code: filterLRpairs(   counts,   min_ligand = 10,   min_receptor = 10,   LR_df = NULL,   species = c("human", "mouse") )

title: Filter ligand-receptor pairs by expression threshold — filterLRpairs
text: counts Gene--bin count matrix (dense sparse). Row names must gene symbols. min_ligand Numeric. Minimum count threshold ligand genes. least one bin must meet exceed value. Default 10. min_receptor Numeric. Minimum count threshold receptor genes. least one bin must meet exceed value. Default 10. LR_df Data frame ligand-receptor pairs columns ligand.symbol receptor.symbol (comma-separated gene symbols multi-subunit complexes). NULL, CellChatDB chosen species downloaded automatically. species Character. CellChatDB download LR_df NULL. One "human" (default) "mouse".

title: Filter ligand-receptor pairs by expression threshold — filterLRpairs
text: subset LR_df containing pairs pass expression thresholds ligand receptor.

title: Filter ligand-receptor pairs by expression threshold — filterLRpairs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Supply a small custom LR_df to avoid a network download LR_df <- data.frame(   ligand.symbol   = c("GENE1", "GENE3"),   receptor.symbol = c("GENE2", "GENE4"),   annotation      = c("Secreted Signaling", "ECM-Receptor"),   row.names       = c("LR1", "LR2") ) set.seed(1) counts <- matrix(   rpois(4 * 50, lambda = c(20, 1, 5, 20)), nrow = 4, ncol = 50,   dimnames = list(c("GENE1", "GENE2", "GENE3", "GENE4"),                   paste0("bin_", 1:50)) ) filterLRpairs(counts, min_ligand = 10, min_receptor = 10, LR_df = LR_df) } # }

what: Bin cells into hexagonal spatial bins — hexBinCells
title: Bin cells into hexagonal spatial bins — hexBinCells
text: Aggregates single-cell spatial data hexagonal bins returns bin-level count matrix together matching sf polygon object, ready pass directly blisa.default.

title: Bin cells into hexagonal spatial bins — hexBinCells
code: hexBinCells(   coords_df,   counts_matrix,   bin_size = 50,   min_cells = 1,   min_total_counts = 10,   group = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Bin cells into hexagonal spatial bins — hexBinCells
text: coords_df Data frame matrix columns x_centroid y_centroid (e.g. output SpatialExperiment::spatialCoords()). Row names must cell IDs matching column names counts_matrix. counts_matrix Gene--cell count matrix (dense sparse). Row names must gene symbols; column names must cell IDs present coords_df. bin_size Numeric. Approximate width hexagonal bin coordinate units (e.g. microns). Analogous grid.length.x sciderHex::gridDensity. Default 50. min_cells Integer. Bins containing fewer min_cells cells dropped output. Default 1. min_total_counts Numeric. Bins whose total counts (summed genes) fall threshold dropped output, alongside min_cells filter. Set 0 disable. Default 10. group Factor character vector length ncol(counts_matrix) giving cell-type label cell. supplied, named list per-cell-type gene--bin matrices included output counts_by_group. Default NULL (computed). verbose Logical. TRUE, print progress messages. Default FALSE (silent).

title: Bin cells into hexagonal spatial bins — hexBinCells
text: list : counts_matrix Gene--bin sparse count matrix (cells combined). Column corresponds row bins. bins sf object hexagonal bin polygons n_cells column recording many cells fall bin total_counts column recording summed counts per bin. Row order matches columns counts_matrix. counts_by_group (group supplied.) named list gene--bin sparse matrices, one per cell-type level, bin order counts_matrix.

title: Bin cells into hexagonal spatial bins — hexBinCells
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(42) n <- 500 coords <- data.frame(   x_centroid = runif(n, 0, 1000),   y_centroid = runif(n, 0, 1000),   row.names  = paste0("cell_", seq_len(n)) ) counts <- Matrix::Matrix(   matrix(rpois(20L * n, lambda = 5), nrow = 20L, ncol = n,          dimnames = list(paste0("gene_", 1:20), paste0("cell_", seq_len(n)))),   sparse = TRUE ) group  <- sample(c("TypeA", "TypeB"), n, replace = TRUE) binned <- hexBinCells(coords, counts, bin_size = 100, group = group) str(binned, max.level = 1) } # }

title: Create a blisa object — new_blisa
text: Constructor blisa S3 class, stores full output blisa.

title: Create a blisa object — new_blisa
code: new_blisa(LR_results, bins, spatial_weights, CCI_scores = NULL)

title: Create a blisa object — new_blisa
text: LR_results Data frame BLISA results, one row per ligand-receptor pair. bins sf object hexagonal bin polygons. spatial_weights Spatial weights list computeSpatialWeights. CCI_scores Wide data frame cell-cell interaction scores runCCI, NULL CCI computed.

title: Create a blisa object — new_blisa
text: object class blisa.

what: Heatmap of CCI scores across all ligand-receptor pairs — plotCCI
title: Heatmap of CCI scores across all ligand-receptor pairs — plotCCI
text: Generic function. Draws clustered heatmap (via ComplexHeatmap) rows Sender \(\rightarrow\) Receiver cell-type pairs columns LR pairs. Row annotations colour-code sender receiver cell types.

title: Heatmap of CCI scores across all ligand-receptor pairs — plotCCI
code: plotCCI(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'blisa' plotCCI(   x,   top_lr = 20,   top_pairs = 30,   lr_pairs = NULL,   sender = NULL,   receiver = NULL,   colors = NULL,   colours = NULL,   main = NULL,   ... )  # Default S3 method plotCCI(   x,   top_lr = 20,   top_pairs = 30,   lr_pairs = NULL,   sender = NULL,   receiver = NULL,   colors = NULL,   colours = NULL,   main = NULL,   ... )

title: Heatmap of CCI scores across all ligand-receptor pairs — plotCCI
text: x blisa object CCI scores data frame (CCI_scores slot blisa object). data frame must contain columns Sender, Receiver, one column per LR pair. ... Additional arguments passed relevant method. top_lr Integer NULL. Number top-ranked LR pairs (sig_numbers) display columns. LR pairs CCI_scores already ordered rank, simply takes first top_lr columns. Ignored lr_pairs supplied. Default 20. top_pairs Integer NULL. Number top sender-receiver pairs display rows, ranked maximum interaction score across displayed LR pairs (top_lr / lr_pairs applied). NULL rows shown. Default 30. lr_pairs Character vector NULL. supplied, LR pair column names shown, order given, overriding top_lr. Names found CCI_scores dropped warning. Default NULL (use top_lr). sender Character vector NULL. provided, rows Sender vector kept. Applied independently receiver (logic supplied). Default NULL (senders). receiver Character vector NULL. provided, rows Receiver vector kept. Applied independently sender (logic supplied). Default NULL (receivers). colors Named character vector mapping cell-type names colours, used sender/receiver row annotations. NULL (default), colours assigned automatically package palette. British spelling colours accepted alias. colours Alias colors (British spelling). Ignored colors supplied. main Character NULL. Title drawn heatmap (mapped column_title ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap). Default NULL (title).

title: Heatmap of CCI scores across all ligand-receptor pairs — plotCCI
text: Invisibly returns Heatmap object.

title: Heatmap of CCI scores across all ligand-receptor pairs — plotCCI
text: plotCCI(blisa): Method blisa object. Extracts CCI_scores delegates plotCCI.default. Stops informative error CCI_scores NULL. plotCCI(default): Method CCI scores data frame (e.g. CCI_scores slot blisa object).

title: Heatmap of CCI scores across all ligand-receptor pairs — plotCCI
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Continuing from the blisa() example: # result <- blisa(spe, bin_size = 50, group = "cell_type") plotCCI(result, top_lr = 20, top_pairs = 30) } # }

what: Sender-by-receiver heatmap of CCI scores for one ligand-receptor pair — plotCCILR
title: Sender-by-receiver heatmap of CCI scores for one ligand-receptor pair — plotCCILR
text: Generic function. Reshapes CCI data frame receiver--sender cell-type matrix one selected LR pair draws clustered heatmap.

title: Sender-by-receiver heatmap of CCI scores for one ligand-receptor pair — plotCCILR
code: plotCCILR(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'blisa' plotCCILR(x, index = 1, ligand = NULL, receptor = NULL, main = NULL, ...)  # Default S3 method plotCCILR(x, lr_pair, main = NULL, ...)

title: Sender-by-receiver heatmap of CCI scores for one ligand-receptor pair — plotCCILR
text: x blisa object CCI scores data frame (CCI_scores slot blisa object). ... Additional arguments passed relevant method. index Integer. Row index LR_results selecting ligand-receptor pair visualise. Ignored ligand receptor supplied. Default 1 (top-ranked pair). ligand Character. Ligand gene symbol. ligand receptor provided matching LR pair located automatically index ignored. Must supplied together receptor. receptor Character. Receptor gene symbol. Must supplied together ligand. main Character NULL. Title drawn heatmap. supplied, heatmap drawn overall title (via ComplexHeatmap::draw); Heatmap object returned invisibly. Default NULL (title; object returned caller print). lr_pair Character. Column name CCI scores data frame corresponding ligand-receptor pair visualise (e.g. "CXCL12_CXCR4").

title: Sender-by-receiver heatmap of CCI scores for one ligand-receptor pair — plotCCILR
text: Heatmap object.

title: Sender-by-receiver heatmap of CCI scores for one ligand-receptor pair — plotCCILR
text: plotCCILR(blisa): Method blisa object. LR pair selected index (default 1, top-ranked pair) unless ligand receptor supplied, case matching row located automatically index ignored. Stops informative error CCI_scores NULL selected LR pair significant hotspots. plotCCILR(default): Method CCI scores data frame (e.g. CCI_scores slot blisa object). LR pair selected column name via lr_pair.

title: Sender-by-receiver heatmap of CCI scores for one ligand-receptor pair — plotCCILR
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Continuing from the blisa() example: # result <- blisa(spe, bin_size = 50, group = "cell_type") plotCCILR(result, index = 1) } # }

what: Spatial map of dominant sender-receiver cell-type pairs at BLISA hotspots — plotCCIspatial
title: Spatial map of dominant sender-receiver cell-type pairs at BLISA hotspots — plotCCIspatial
text: selected ligand-receptor pair, identifies dominant interacting cell-type pair significant hotspot bin draws spatial map tissue coloured pairs. Receiver cells inside hotspot bins; sender cells drawn immediate neighbourhood.

title: Spatial map of dominant sender-receiver cell-type pairs at BLISA hotspots — plotCCIspatial
code: plotCCIspatial(   x,   counts_by_group,   index = 1,   ligand = NULL,   receptor = NULL,   top_pairs = 30 )

title: Spatial map of dominant sender-receiver cell-type pairs at BLISA hotspots — plotCCIspatial
text: x blisa object returned blisa. counts_by_group Named list gene--bin count matrices, one per cell type. Typically counts_by_group element returned hexBinCells. Names must match cell-type levels. index Integer. Row index x$LR_results selecting ligand-receptor pair visualise. Ignored ligand receptor supplied. Default 1 (top-ranked pair). ligand Character. Ligand gene symbol. ligand receptor provided matching LR pair located automatically index ignored. Must supplied together receptor. receptor Character. Receptor gene symbol. Must supplied together ligand. top_pairs Integer. Maximum number distinct cell-type pairs show legend; remaining pairs grouped "rare pairs". Default 30.

title: Spatial map of dominant sender-receiver cell-type pairs at BLISA hotspots — plotCCIspatial
text: ggplot object.

title: Spatial map of dominant sender-receiver cell-type pairs at BLISA hotspots — plotCCIspatial
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Continuing from the blisa() example: # result <- blisa(spe, bin_size = 50, group = "cell_type") binned <- hexBinCells(   as.data.frame(SpatialExperiment::spatialCoords(spe)),   SummarizedExperiment::assay(spe, "counts"),   bin_size = 50, group = spe$cell_type ) plotCCIspatial(result, binned$counts_by_group, index = 1) } # }

what: Sender-by-receiver heatmap of aggregated CCI scores across LR pairs — plotCCIsummary
title: Sender-by-receiver heatmap of aggregated CCI scores across LR pairs — plotCCIsummary
text: Generic function. Aggregates CCI scores across (top-ranked) ligand-receptor pairs draws clustered receiver--sender heatmap, one cell per Sender \(\rightarrow\) Receiver combination.

title: Sender-by-receiver heatmap of aggregated CCI scores across LR pairs — plotCCIsummary
code: plotCCIsummary(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'blisa' plotCCIsummary(   x,   top_lr = NULL,   sender = NULL,   receiver = NULL,   agg_fun = sum,   main = NULL,   ... )  # Default S3 method plotCCIsummary(   x,   top_lr = NULL,   sender = NULL,   receiver = NULL,   agg_fun = sum,   main = NULL,   ... )

title: Sender-by-receiver heatmap of aggregated CCI scores across LR pairs — plotCCIsummary
text: x blisa object CCI scores data frame (CCI_scores slot blisa object). ... Additional arguments passed relevant method. top_lr Integer NULL. Number top-ranked LR pairs (sig_numbers) include aggregating. LR pairs CCI_scores already ordered rank, takes first top_lr columns. NULL (default) uses pairs. sender Character vector NULL. provided, rows Sender vector kept (logic receiver). Default NULL (senders). receiver Character vector NULL. provided, rows Receiver vector kept (logic sender). Default NULL (receivers). agg_fun Function used aggregate scores across LR pairs Sender \(\rightarrow\) Receiver combination. Receives numeric vector NAs already removed. Default sum. main Character NULL. Title drawn heatmap. supplied, heatmap drawn overall title (via ComplexHeatmap::draw); Heatmap object returned invisibly. Default NULL (title; object returned caller print).

title: Sender-by-receiver heatmap of aggregated CCI scores across LR pairs — plotCCIsummary
text: Heatmap object.

title: Sender-by-receiver heatmap of aggregated CCI scores across LR pairs — plotCCIsummary
text: plotCCIsummary(blisa): Method blisa object. Stops informative error CCI_scores NULL. plotCCIsummary(default): Method CCI scores data frame (e.g. CCI_scores slot blisa object).

title: Sender-by-receiver heatmap of aggregated CCI scores across LR pairs — plotCCIsummary
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Continuing from the blisa() example: # result <- blisa(spe, bin_size = 50, group = "cell_type") plotCCIsummary(result) plotCCIsummary(result, top_lr = 10, agg_fun = mean) } # }

what: Spatial hotspot map for one ligand-receptor pair — plotHotspots
title: Spatial hotspot map for one ligand-receptor pair — plotHotspots
text: Generic function. Plots bin coloured significance status: empty, non-significant, significant hotspot (continuous gradient -log10 p-value 1 - p-value).

title: Spatial hotspot map for one ligand-receptor pair — plotHotspots
code: plotHotspots(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'blisa' plotHotspots(   x,   index = 1,   ligand = NULL,   receptor = NULL,   log_pval = TRUE,   p_cutoff = NULL,   ... )

title: Spatial hotspot map for one ligand-receptor pair — plotHotspots
text: x blisa object. ... Additional arguments passed method. index Integer. Row index LR_results selecting ligand-receptor pair visualise. Ignored ligand receptor supplied. Default 1 (top-ranked pair). ligand Character. Ligand gene symbol. ligand receptor provided matching LR pair located automatically index ignored. Must supplied together receptor. receptor Character. Receptor gene symbol. Must supplied together ligand. log_pval Logical. TRUE (default), colour significant bins -log10(p-value). FALSE, use 1 - p-value. p_cutoff Numeric NULL. NULL (default), pre-computed hotspot bins stored blisa object used, reflecting p_cutoff High-High quadrant classification applied blisa. numeric value supplied, bins re-defined fly all_pval <= p_cutoff quadrant label "High-High" (stored all_quadrant), giving exact re-threshold consistent original classification.

title: Spatial hotspot map for one ligand-receptor pair — plotHotspots
text: ggplot object.

title: Spatial hotspot map for one ligand-receptor pair — plotHotspots
text: plotHotspots(blisa): Method blisa object.

title: Spatial hotspot map for one ligand-receptor pair — plotHotspots
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Continuing from the blisa() example: # result <- blisa(spe, bin_size = 50, group = "cell_type") plotHotspots(result, index = 1) plotHotspots(result, index = 1, log_pval = FALSE) } # }

what: Dot plot ranking LR pairs by number of significant hotspot bins — plotLRrank
title: Dot plot ranking LR pairs by number of significant hotspot bins — plotLRrank
text: Generic function ranking LR pairs. Dispatches class x: plotLRrank.blisa accepts blisa object uses LR_results slot directly. plotLRrank.data.frame accepts LR_results data frame directly.

title: Dot plot ranking LR pairs by number of significant hotspot bins — plotLRrank
code: plotLRrank(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'blisa' plotLRrank(x, top = 30, pt_size = 4, flip = FALSE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'data.frame' plotLRrank(x, top = 30, pt_size = 4, flip = FALSE, ...)

title: Dot plot ranking LR pairs by number of significant hotspot bins — plotLRrank
text: x blisa object data frame LR results. data frame must contain columns sig_numbers annotation. ... Additional arguments passed relevant method. top Integer NULL. Number top LR pairs (sig_numbers) display. Default 30. pt_size Numeric. Point size passed geom_point. Default 4. flip Logical. TRUE, LR pairs placed x-axis hotspot count y-axis (vertical orientation). Default FALSE (LR pairs y-axis, horizontal orientation).

title: Dot plot ranking LR pairs by number of significant hotspot bins — plotLRrank
text: ggplot object.

title: Dot plot ranking LR pairs by number of significant hotspot bins — plotLRrank
text: plotLRrank(blisa): Method blisa object. Extracts LR_results delegates plotLRrank.data.frame. plotLRrank(data.frame): Method data frame LR results (e.g. LR_results slot blisa object).

title: Dot plot ranking LR pairs by number of significant hotspot bins — plotLRrank
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Continuing from the blisa() example: # result <- blisa(spe, bin_size = 50, group = "cell_type") plotLRrank(result, top = 30) plotLRrank(result, top = 20, flip = TRUE) } # }

what: Score cell-cell interactions from BLISA hotspots — runCCI
title: Score cell-cell interactions from BLISA hotspots — runCCI
text: Generic function scoring cell-cell interactions. Dispatches class x: runCCI.blisa accepts blisa object. CCI_scores already present overwrite = FALSE (default), object returned unchanged. Set overwrite = TRUE counts_by_group recompute replace existing scores. scores exist, counts_by_group must supplied scores computed attached. runCCI.default performs raw computation given blisa object counts_by_group list, returning scores data frame. Used internally runCCI.blisa blisa.default.

title: Score cell-cell interactions from BLISA hotspots — runCCI
code: runCCI(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'blisa' runCCI(x, counts_by_group = NULL, overwrite = FALSE, ...)  # Default S3 method runCCI(x, counts_by_group, ...)

title: Score cell-cell interactions from BLISA hotspots — runCCI
text: x blisa object. ... Additional arguments passed relevant method. counts_by_group Named list gene--bin sparse count matrices, one per group level (e.g. cell type). Typically counts_by_group element list returned hexBinCells group supplied. Names must match group levels. Required x$CCI_scores NULL overwrite = TRUE. overwrite Logical. FALSE (default) x$CCI_scores already populated, object returned unchanged. TRUE counts_by_group supplied, existing scores recomputed replaced.

title: Score cell-cell interactions from BLISA hotspots — runCCI
text: See individual method documentation. runCCI.blisa: input blisa object CCI_scores populated (wide data frame – rows "Sender->Receiver" group pairs, columns LR pairs). runCCI.default: data frame "Sender->Receiver" row names one column per significant LR pair containing interaction score 0.5 * log2(receiver * sender + 1).

title: Score cell-cell interactions from BLISA hotspots — runCCI
text: runCCI(blisa): Method blisa object. CCI_scores already present overwrite = FALSE (default), object returned unchanged. Set overwrite = TRUE counts_by_group recompute replace existing scores. scores exist, counts_by_group must supplied scores computed attached x$CCI_scores. runCCI(default): Default method. Performs raw CCI computation returns scores data frame. Typically called internally; use runCCI.blisa compute attach scores blisa object one step.

title: Score cell-cell interactions from BLISA hotspots — runCCI
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Continuing from the blisa() example: # result <- blisa(spe, bin_size = 50, group = "cell_type")  # CCI is computed automatically when group is supplied to blisa(); # access the scores directly: head(result$CCI_scores)  # Or compute / recompute scores explicitly: binned <- hexBinCells(   as.data.frame(SpatialExperiment::spatialCoords(spe)),   SummarizedExperiment::assay(spe, "counts"),   bin_size = 50, group = spe$cell_type ) result2 <- runCCI(result, counts_by_group = binned$counts_by_group,                   overwrite = TRUE) } # }

title: blisa 0.2.3
text: LR_results row names now informative IDs built ligand/receptor symbols, subunits joined | two sides _ (e.g. TGFB1_TGFBR2|TGFBR1). original CellChatDB interaction_name retained new interaction_name column. plotCCIspatial() now handles multi-subunit ligand/receptor complexes (previously errored subscript bounds). plotHotspots(), plotCCIspatial(), plotCCILR() now match ligand/receptor arguments subunit set, independent order, separator (,, _, |), whitespace.

title: blisa 0.2.2
text: Added fast = TRUE option blisa() uses fastLISA::local_moran_bv (C/OpenMP backend) instead spdep::localmoran_bv bivariate local Moran’s computation. Default behaviour unchanged. New cpu_threads argument controlling parallelism fast = TRUE.

title: blisa 0.2.1
text: Update vignettes.

title: blisa 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-02 Initial CRAN release.
